framework.name: AP2 Cryptographic Consent = Human-in-the-Loop by Design
framework.description: Google's Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) requires cryptographic proof that a human approved every transaction. This is Google explicitly choosing the per-transaction permission side of the split, not portable pre-auth. The biggest player in the room is betting on human-in-the-loop.
framework.raw_quote: Through Google's Agent Payments Protocol (AP2), every transaction includes proof that a human approved it. Regulators and merchants both want to know: did a real person authorize this purchase?

source.title: Google's Universal Commerce Protocol: What It Means for Payment ...
